content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

All three amendments identify genuine defects in the milestone planning flow. Amendment fr-5-7597c84a identifies a high-severity crash-recovery gap where a __starting__ reservation permanently wedges planning. Amendment fr-5-9618b042 correctly identifies that replan status is invisible to users. Amendment fr-5-b5b04d7d catches overly broad error handling that silently destroys data on corruption. All accepted.

## Votes

- `fr-5-7597c84a`: Accept
  Verified: reserve_pending_requirements_run (line 733) writes a __starting__:<pid>-<ts> marker into pending_requirements_run_id. The real run_id only replaces this marker at line 682-688 via replace_pending_requirements_run, which fires after draft_milestone completes the full pipeline (service.rs:188-217). If the process is killed between line 734 (snapshot write with __starting__ marker) and line 688 (replace), the marker persists. On the next invocation, reserve_pending_requirements_run at lines 723-728 detects the __starting__ prefix and returns a hard error with no recovery path — the process ID and timestamp embedded in the reservation belong to a dead process, yet nothing ever clears it. The load_or_start loop (line 572) only clears stale runs via load_pending_requirements_run, but it never reaches that path because the __starting__ marker causes a bail-out before the Existing branch is tried. This is a real crash-recovery defect that permanently wedges milestone planning without manual file editing.
- `fr-5-9618b042`: Accept
  Verified: MilestoneSummaryView (lines 61-72) and MilestoneDetailView (lines 74-90) both serialize snapshot.status directly and neither includes pending_requirements_run_id. When replanning a milestone that is already 'ready' or 'completed', reserve_pending_requirements_run sets the pending pointer (line 734) but never transitions snapshot.status from its current value. If the requirements run enters AwaitingAnswers, handle_plan prints a message and returns (lines 156-161) but leaves status unchanged. Subsequent milestone show/status calls will display 'ready' or 'completed' while a replan is blocked on clarification. This is a genuine UX defect — the user has no way to discover the pending replan state through the standard milestone commands.
- `fr-5-b5b04d7d`: Accept
  Verified: load_pending_requirements_run at lines 615-620 catches all Err(_) from read_run and clears the pending pointer unconditionally. However, read_run in fs.rs at lines 5427-5441 distinguishes three failure modes: (1) NotFound → InvalidRequirementsState, (2) deserialization failure → CorruptRecord, (3) other I/O errors → generic. All three are lumped together by the Err(_) catch, meaning a corrupt run.json or a transient I/O error (permissions, disk full) will silently discard the pending pointer and orphan the existing requirements run data. The code should only clear the pointer for the not-found case; corruption and I/O errors should propagate so the operator can investigate and repair rather than silently forking a new run that abandons prior clarification data.
